summary: Validated staging wrapper regression for preview: public root loads, local proxy (3000) serves backend-rendered login HTML, backend wrapper serves app on 8001, login UI is reachable and functional, and full backend pytest suite passes.

ui_bugs.issue: Password input and Authenticate submit button do not expose data-testid attributes, reducing automated test reliability.

issues: Footer helper text uses very low-contrast gray on dark background and is hard to read.

action_items: Add data-testid on login password input and authenticate button (and other key interactive controls) for stable e2e coverage.
Optional: improve login footer text contrast for accessibility.
critical_code_review_comments: frontend/server.js uses fallback defaults for PORT/HOST; if strict fail-fast env behavior is required across environments, remove defaults and require explicit env values.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified via GET that preview URL, localhost:3000 proxy, and localhost:8001 backend all return 200 and matching login HTML. Browser automation confirmed successful login -> dashboard -> logout flow on preview URL.
rca of the issue: Original staging responsiveness concern is not reproducible now. Wrapper setup appears correct: backend wrapper imports app.main app object and frontend proxy forwards root traffic to backend target; both services are RUNNING under supervisor and respond with expected HTML/pages.
